prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sampled commercial begins with an almost entirely dark frame. No people, objects, setting, or readable text are visible at the opening frame, apart from a faint broadcast-style watermark or logo in the lower-right corner.

By the final sampled frame, the scene has changed to what appears to be an automotive parts or dealership counter/display area. A man wearing a light-colored lab coat or work coat stands behind a counter filled with assorted automotive product containers, including bottles, jugs, cans, and boxes. Behind him is a large sign reading “PARTS TO GO,” with a small GM-style logo at the left of the sign. More product displays and shelves are visible in the background.

Large on-screen text appears across the lower portion of the frame: “WE GOT IT.” Beneath it, smaller blue text reads “At Your GM Dealer.” The visual emphasis is on availability of parts/products at a GM dealer, supported by the counter full of branded-looking containers and the dealership parts signage.

Because only the first and last frames are provided, the intervening action, camera movement, and any transformations between the dark opening and the dealership-parts display are not visible. No dialogue, music, or voice-over can be inferred from the frames.
